230403.explanation: Good integration does not have to cost the municipality more money. We are proud of our Montferland culture. We expect newcomers to learn our language and immerse themselves in our culture and society. We base this on mutual respect. That really doesn't have to cost more money.

230404.explanation: We accommodate people who come to the Netherlands to work here for a short term, preferably as close as possible to their workplace. There are certainly rules regarding the welfare of the employees.

230405.explanation: This statement goes too far for us. Of course, we must eventually transition to a more sustainable economy. We want to encourage that, not 'preach' it.

230406.explanation: We remain down-to-earth and realistic: we do not favor fields full of solar panels. Especially since there is still plenty of space on roofs to place them, and many more roofs will be built in the coming years.

230407.explanation: This is already the case in Montferland. Sports are important, but simply being able to participate with peers of the same age is even more important. We maintain existing programs such as the participation scheme so all children can join in.

230408.explanation: In recent years, we have already significantly expanded our municipal enforcement officers (boa) capacity. The question is whether even more capacity will improve safety.

230409.explanation: At this moment, we see no reason to specifically implement camera surveillance in Didam. We are open to discussing this if there is a demand for it.

230411.explanation: We must do everything legally possible to give priority to our residents who want to stay here when it comes to housing, including social rental housing.

230412.explanation: We adhere to the national agreement to build at least 30% social rental housing as a municipality. However, we build according to demand and believe this percentage may differ per locality depending on greater or lesser need. Every locality should have a healthy mix of rental and owner-occupied homes.

230413.explanation: Not every location is suitable for high-rise buildings. Where it fits, taller construction can be a solution to create more living space. We do not want to build everything densely without leaving room and greenery to live together.

230414.explanation: We currently use 120% of the minimum income as the poverty line. Everyone earning less than that 120% receives (financial) support; we want to maintain this threshold. There must be a healthy balance between the government as a safety net and individual strength.

230416.explanation: We have great respect for caregivers. Whether it is possible to work alongside caregiving duties varies greatly depending on the situation.

230417.explanation: No. Youth work is an investment in the future. It is important to support this group during a vulnerable phase of their lives.

230419.explanation: Where possible, we want to encourage residents to install charging points on their own property. We want to continue meeting the demand for public charging stations in public spaces. We do not want to force electric driving but facilitate the demand.

230420.explanation: This is not necessary everywhere. There are places where it is logical and necessary, but in other places, 50 km/h is fine if the street and buildings allow it.

230421.explanation: Temporary debt assistance under clear conditions can help an entrepreneur get through a difficult period without immediately having to close the business. Appropriate debt support keeps the business operational, preserves jobs, and offers the entrepreneur new prospects.

230422.explanation: The child mayor is a wonderful initiative to engage children in thinking about their municipality. Children inspire each other through this. We believe more attention should be given to it.

230423.explanation: We have existing neighborhood and village councils that are connected to the municipality. They generally function well and are specifically involved in themes relevant to neighborhoods, villages and the town. We prefer to leave this to the needs of each village, neighborhood or town.

230424.explanation: Questions to the municipality must be answered as quickly as possible. We believe you should receive at least an acknowledgment of receipt within one working day, including an estimate of the response time. Complex questions are better answered thoroughly and attentively than hastily.

230425.explanation: As agreed in the Environmental Vision, the expansion east of ’s-Heerenberg will be our final XXL logistics expansion. Looking further ahead, we will focus on our small and medium enterprises, with special attention to local manufacturing.

230426.explanation: Montferland is beautiful. We want to showcase this together with the Achterhoek region. Tourism benefits our local entrepreneurs, but we do not want mass tourism. Tourism must suit the size and culture of Montferland.

230427.explanation: To retain young people in our municipality, the manufacturing industry is essential and must, together with knowledge and innovation, ensure future-proof employment. This way, we ensure young people can continue to live and work here!

230428.explanation: We prefer to work with local companies. When our local companies offer the best price-quality ratio, we will give them priority where legally allowed.

230429.explanation: We are open to considering this if there is a need among our residents.

230430.explanation: We do not want to provide shelter spots as quickly as possible but within the timeframe legally imposed by the minister. We prefer well-thought-out shelter over hasty urgency.

230431.explanation: We prefer small-scale shelter rather than housing large groups of 300 people as the COA promotes. The maximum number of people may vary per location.

230432.explanation: We prefer to first use other alternatives to generate energy rather than installing wind turbines.

230433.explanation: The Bergherbos is not a suitable location for wind turbines in our view. Moreover, we prefer first to use other alternatives for energy generation before placing wind turbines.

230434.explanation: We want to give village halls more space without them competing with existing hospitality businesses. Village halls are intended as accessible and social meeting places for a community, not as locations for commercial parties and events.

230435.explanation: In the Groenlo agreement, 2050 is the target year to be energy neutral. Anything earlier is nice but should not come at the expense of, for example, employment, nature, or landscape if it is not yet necessary.

230436.explanation: We find it important that children grow up in a green environment. We gladly encourage greener schoolyards that provide more play inspiration and shade on warm days.

230437.explanation: Montferland is a green municipality. We are proud of that. We already have many trees in Montferland, and due to an adopted proposal, 2000 more are currently being planted.

230438.explanation: We are not opposed to electric driving. Where possible, we want to encourage residents to install charging points on their own property. We want to continue meeting the demand for charging stations in public spaces. We do not want to enforce electric driving but definitely want to facilitate the demand.

230439.explanation: We believe it is good that every young person comes into contact with music, music education and culture. The way culture and music are currently offered in our municipality meets this.

230443.explanation: By imposing a percentage, the percentage becomes the goal. We do not favor that. We prefer to look at other ways to stimulate art.

230445.explanation: We want to create housing as much and as quickly as carefully possible. However, we prefer first to engage with people to understand the reasons for vacancy before the municipality starts issuing fines.

230446.explanation: We believe that a new home nowadays should be able to be energy neutral.

230448.explanation: We build according to need, not based on a fixed percentage. We build generation-proof homes, so there are more houses for young people, couples, and seniors alike, allowing the housing market to flow better again.

230449.explanation: We can imagine that when you leave a larger social rental home to live smaller, you also pay less net. For owner-occupied homes, we prefer to ensure there are enough suitable affordable (senior) homes.

230450.explanation: We see no reason why students from any type of education should not be able to do internships with the municipality in the same way. We like to focus on the qualities someone brings.

230452.explanation: We initiated the motion calling for this and therefore believe it is necessary. In practice, there are currently not enough parking spaces, which leads to frustration among visitors and residents. We need to solve this.

230453.explanation: We basically think this is a good idea. However, we would like to look at where this is most suitable and safe in our municipality. We believe this should be done step by step, for example when lighting is due for replacement.

230455.explanation: We do not want our residents to become isolated. From a spirit of neighborliness, we want to pay attention to each other. If a municipal contact person can support this, we are happy to do so as a municipality.

230456.explanation: We prefer to first see if we can organize home care differently before spending more money. More funding is not always the solution or the need.

288608.explanation: Swimming lessons are very costly. As long as the municipality does not receive funds from the national government for this, we see no possibility to offer swimming lessons free of charge to all children. We choose to support children from families who cannot afford swimming lessons. Schools also indicate they have too little time for school swimming, alongside all their other tasks.

288609.explanation: The property tax (ozb) must be lowered. In many municipalities, it has become 'normal' to raise the ozb sharply in recent years. In Montferland, we do not do this. We are proud of that. If it were possible to lower the ozb, we would have done so. Unfortunately, this is not realistic when all costs are only rising.

288610.explanation: We look at the qualities of caregivers, regardless of their background. We expect caregivers to respect every culture. There is a large staff shortage in care, making it seem impossible to meet this expectation.

288611.explanation: We see a need for locations for our (SME) businesses. In addition to creating new locations, we also want to keep our current business parks lively to prevent vacancies there.

288612.explanation: The municipality must continue using existing school buildings. In consultation with school boards, the most suitable solution for a school will be sought. This can be reuse, but in some cases also new construction.

288614.explanation: The library has already had to make significant budget cuts over the past years and has just started a merger process. Let's ensure calm, clarity, and a future perspective so we can keep a library in our major centers.

230403.explanation: As far as Helder is concerned, more than enough money is already spent on integration. The willingness to integrate is not dependent on money!

230404.explanation: Absolutely agree; this also prevents additional pressure on the housing market for our own residents.

230405.explanation: That makes life even harder for entrepreneurs and products even more expensive.

230406.explanation: As long as not all usable roofs in the entirety of Montferland are used for solar panels, Helder does not want agricultural land to be made available for solar energy.

230407.explanation: Every child must be able to participate in sports; fortunately, many such schemes already exist to make sports accessible.

230408.explanation: For Helder, a safe living environment is very important. Tackling undermining crime is also a high priority for us. Community enforcement officers (BOAs) can play a role in this, as long as they are authorized and trained. It is also very important that signals from residents are picked up and thoroughly analyzed. Good information provision and data sharing are therefore high on our agenda.

230409.explanation: Of course, if there is frequent nuisance in a fixed location, cameras are fine, but we are against structural camera surveillance.

230411.explanation: Yes, absolutely. It is in our program, and we also submitted a motion for it last year. For Helder, our own residents come first!

230412.explanation: Build according to need, then the numbers will become clear automatically. Flow-through is important here.

230413.explanation: Not only in Didam but in the whole of Montferland, that is not possible. In this rural and tourist environment, three is really the maximum.

230414.explanation: Do not tamper with these existing rules.

230416.explanation: We are getting more and more informal caregivers, but everything must remain affordable.

230417.explanation: Youth work is important, and cuts in this area should be well considered as much as possible.

230419.explanation: This question is asked far too generally. If the question is about more charging points near recreational areas, then we say a resounding yes. If it is in a neighborhood with only detached houses, our answer is no.

230420.explanation: The situation as it currently is in Montferland is fine with us.

230421.explanation: Every entrepreneur bears entrepreneurial risk. The government does not have to carry that risk.

230422.explanation: No, it is really great how the child mayors take on their roles and add something, especially as role models for many children.

230423.explanation: The village councils are often deeply rooted in society; they are the eyes and ears of their community and know exactly what is going on.

230424.explanation: One day may be very quick, but the existing six weeks as it is now is far too long!

230425.explanation: Yes, absolutely. Apart from many labor migrants and the resulting pressure on housing, these (large) halls do not really fit in our beautiful, tourist environment.

230426.explanation: Certainly, tourism brings us a lot. Conduct these advertising campaigns together with local entrepreneurs, just as the municipality should try to spend all its expenses locally as much as possible.

230427.explanation: Yes, absolutely. This sector provides a lot of employment, especially for our own youth.

230428.explanation: Yes, absolutely. For Helder, no discussion at all: just like our residents, our entrepreneurs always come first!

230429.explanation: The municipality must be able to cooperate with any municipality to organize something jointly. So not only with German municipalities. Furthermore, you should only organize something if there is demand for it.

230430.explanation: Helder is against large-scale asylum seeker reception. Every decision about this must have broad support from residents and especially neighbors.

230431.explanation: Helder will only consider this with a well-considered and supported decision from the population. That is not the case now! In addition, the management agreement with the COA will play an important role.

230432.explanation: As far as we're concerned, wind turbines belong offshore, where there is more wind and space.

230433.explanation: In our opinion, this question needs no further explanation. Wind turbines belong at sea, not in the Bergherbos.

230434.explanation: If there is no nuisance for the surroundings and it does not hinder a local hospitality venue, then Helder is in favor.

230435.explanation: The idea is of course fine, but the infrastructure makes this impossible and it still costs far too much.

230436.explanation: If there is space, playing in a green environment is of course fine. But it must never be at the expense of play space.

230437.explanation: We are approximately the greenest municipality in the Netherlands and at this moment, 2000 (!) additional trees are being planted.

230438.explanation: The municipality may calmly cooperate in issuing permits for this.

230439.explanation: It’s all wonderful, but where in this question does the need appear?

230443.explanation: It’s all wonderful, but where does the need appear in this question?

230445.explanation: Agreed, but it must be demonstrated that it is outside the owner's fault. We mean, for example, when a permit is delayed longer than expected.

230446.explanation: It is up to the owner how they manage their energy, not the government. Conditions for self-sufficient energy generation have proven to be unreliable, think for example of the disappearance of the net metering scheme for solar panels.

230448.explanation: As with all housing types, building should be based on need. Mobility can make more senior housing necessary.

230449.explanation: This depends on the housing market; under most circumstances, a large family home yields more than enough (equity) to cover moving costs.

230450.explanation: Certainly, there is a great need for internships, so the municipality must also offer this opportunity.

230451.explanation: As far as Helder is concerned, the old city area may be made car-restricted, but the center itself must remain accessible. Helder strongly supports relocating Lidl to 's-Heerenberg-Oost. This will only bring benefits.

230452.explanation: At this moment, there are sufficient parking options at Nevelhorst itself. There is a great need for parking around the Magriethal.

230453.explanation: A great plan. It will improve safety and comfort.

230455.explanation: The implementation may be difficult, but you cannot be against this.

230456.explanation: Difficult to answer; it must really be addressed individually. If there is a real need for more individual home care, then it should be considered. Extra expenses, of course, will come from somewhere else.

288608.explanation: Absolutely, all our children must have the opportunity to take swimming lessons; if this happens through school swimming, that is absolutely good.

288609.explanation: Absolutely, the costs for many of our residents are too high. Lowering property tax helps many residents.

288610.explanation: As much as possible, existing care providers must be utilized; otherwise, care will become even more unaffordable.

288611.explanation: Current existing business parks may need to be redesigned or adjusted. Montferland already has many large business parks, especially near Azewijn.

288612.explanation: Of course, as a school, provided they meet the requirements for a healthy living environment. But if the school has moved, another communal use might also be found.

288614.explanation: Of course agreed, reading is the foundation for much knowledge and must remain accessible or affordable for every resident.

230403.explanation: Integration is important, but mainly requires effectiveness, customization, and support. More money is not an end in itself; first, it must be clear what really works.

230404.explanation: Cooperation is only possible if housing meets quality standards and nuisance is prevented. We oppose the dependency whereby labor migrants lose their homes immediately upon job loss. A labor migrant hotel can be part of the solution.

230405.explanation: It is not possible for every company to operate sustainably.

230407.explanation: Every child should be able to play sports. Through schemes like the 'Meedoen-regeling', the municipality ensures that financial barriers do not prevent participation.

230408.explanation: If you set rules, you must also be able to enforce them. This happens too little currently. Consider, for example, the nuisance in some neighborhoods within our municipality.

230409.explanation: In places where safety is under pressure, camera surveillance can be an effective tool. This must always be used carefully, respecting privacy and as part of a broader safety approach.

230411.explanation: As far as possible within the laws and regulations.

230412.explanation: This way we ensure a balanced housing stock and keep Montferland accessible for starters, families, and seniors. We also prevent the centralization of social rental homes in large centers and avoid a one-sided composition of neighborhoods.

230413.explanation: We believe you cannot avoid this within the large centers if you want to house everyone.

230414.explanation: Poverty reduction requires customization and solidarity. Lowering the income threshold helps fewer people who need it, while early support can prevent bigger problems.

230416.explanation: Dependent on the intensity of the care they provide. We consider this very important work.

230417.explanation: That is 'putting the cart before the horse,' which can cause problems with enforcement.

230419.explanation: With the caveat that network congestion must first decrease.

230420.explanation: This increases traffic safety, reduces noise pollution, and makes residential neighborhoods more pleasant and safer for everyone.

230421.explanation: By helping local entrepreneurs without forcing them to give up their business immediately, we preserve employment and strengthen the local economy.

230422.explanation: The child mayor does very good work regarding youth. He/she is essentially the voice of young people.

230423.explanation: Village councils ensure involvement, short communication lines, and policies that better align with the concerns of neighborhoods and districts.

230424.explanation: Within one working day is not realistic, but the municipality must quickly clarify when an answer will follow.

230425.explanation: The local economy gains nothing from these building blocks.

230426.explanation: Touristic promotion is primarily a regional task. The municipality of Montferland should collaborate and connect in this, but should not start its own costly advertising campaign.

230427.explanation: This offers prospects for young people, ensures sustainable employment, and strengthens Montferland's economic future. Additionally, the manufacturing industry provides innovation and high-quality employment.

230428.explanation: Provided this can be done within the applicable procurement procedures.

230429.explanation: Cooperation with German neighboring municipalities can strengthen local culture and create connections, provided it complements existing initiatives and has local support.

230430.explanation: The municipality must comply with the law, but carefulness, support, and preconditions are decisive for us.

230431.explanation: Provided the costs are still fully paid by the COA.

230432.explanation: We believe no more wind turbines should be added at all.

230433.explanation: The Bergherbos is a beautiful piece of nature in Montferland; we absolutely do not want wind turbines there.

230434.explanation: Community centers should be given more space to organize activities and generate income within clear agreements, as long as this fits the local situation and does not cause unfair competition.

230435.explanation: We work towards energy saving and generation but consider 2035 not realistic for Montferland.

230436.explanation: Several projects are already underway here to relocate seedlings. Schools could participate in these.

230437.explanation: Greening is important but must be done carefully and appropriately. It’s about the right trees in the right place, not numbers alone.

230438.explanation: You will need these for electrification anyway.

230439.explanation: Culture and music stimulate creativity, self-development, and connections among young people.

230443.explanation: That is a very strict statement. Let's first see what this means for the municipal budget. And if it concerns a private building, we cannot demand this.

230445.explanation: This infringes property rights. You can invite the owner to ask why it is vacant.

230446.explanation: The municipality encourages sustainable new construction but has limited legal means to mandate full energy neutrality. New buildings must be future-proof and energy-efficient.

230448.explanation: Flow-through and suitable housing for the elderly are important but require customized solutions per village and project. A fixed percentage for all new construction is too rigid and does not do justice to local differences.

230449.explanation: Moving should be attractive, but that requires suitable homes and good support, not just a financial incentive, which may lead to inequality in implementation.

230450.explanation: MBO vocational graduates are increasingly needed, so the municipality should set a good example in this itself.

230451.explanation: Good accessibility is important for residents, visitors, and entrepreneurs and contributes to a vibrant center.

230452.explanation: Good parking facilities are necessary to make the Nevelhorst safe and accessible for all visitors. We also insist that these facilities remain free of charge.

230453.explanation: Smart lighting increases safety, limits energy use, and reduces light pollution.

230455.explanation: Often neighbors or foundations notice signs of concern sooner than children or partners alone. This signaling must therefore be easily accessible, and early detection helps prevent loneliness and ensures support reaches the right place sooner.

230456.explanation: If this number increases, personnel problems will arise. It is already often difficult to arrange good care for every elderly person.

288608.explanation: The municipality must (again) provide funding for school swimming. School swimming contributes to children’s safety and health and ensures every child learns to swim regardless of home situation. The municipality plays an important preventive role here.

288609.explanation: We find affordability important but also believe the municipality must remain financially healthy. Lowering property tax is only justifiable if it does not come at the expense of facilities and support for residents.

288610.explanation: Good care connects to the person behind the care request. Freedom of choice increases trust and quality of care, provided it is within the applicable laws, regulations, and quality standards.

288611.explanation: Economic development is important but must be carefully weighed against livability, landscape, and traffic pressure. Expansion of industrial sites is only desirable if there is demonstrable need and it is well integrated. Absolutely no more XXL logistics.

288612.explanation: Continuing to use existing school buildings is logical where they meet safety, sustainability, and educational requirements. At the same time, customization must remain possible when new construction or renovation better fits the future.

288614.explanation: The library is more than a lending place: it is an important facility for education, meeting, and accessible support. Cutting back here affects those residents who need it most.

230403.explanation: Not spending more money but using and applying the available budget with clearly described end goals.

230404.explanation: But within the Roemer standard. And with enforcement efforts.

230405.explanation: We choose voluntary transition for companies.

230406.explanation: Don't ruin our countryside. There are enough possibilities elsewhere, for example, on business rooftops.

230407.explanation: The municipality is already doing this, 'participate' scheme.

230408.explanation: Not only with safety but with overall enforcement. Also considering the decreasing police deployment.

230409.explanation: Based on incidents, this should apply to all of Montferland.

230411.explanation: But limited by existing laws and regulations.

230414.explanation: Is existing policy and should not be changed.

230416.explanation: Someone who works does this alongside their regular job as well.

230419.explanation: But only in places outside the center, e.g. Gouden Hands or sports fields.

230421.explanation: There is a legal regulation BBZA.

230422.explanation: It is important that youth are involved in local democracy.

230423.explanation: Important in the context of participation.

230424.explanation: It is important to stay in contact. We want to improve services.

230425.explanation: No added value in the current municipality. The current situation is sufficient.

230427.explanation: There is a need partly due to the education level and to have young people also live in Montferland.

230428.explanation: But we must stay price-conscious. Tendering standards must also fit.

230429.explanation: Let society take the initiative if there is a need via Euregio or other agencies.

230430.explanation: We enforce the law. And if urgency is required, we will act.

230431.explanation: Also depends on location and enforcement.

230433.explanation: It is known where they will be located.

230434.explanation: It must fit within the current permit. Small-scale hospitality is possible now, also considering subsidies. No para-commercial activities.

230435.explanation: Disagree because not everyone can afford it. Housing associations should only allow rent increases once homes have been made sustainable.

230437.explanation: We have made up ground in recent years.

230438.explanation: The design of public spaces must be taken into account.

230443.explanation: If desired, include specific regulations. Determine situationally.

230445.explanation: There must be a legal basis for it.

230446.explanation: New construction must primarily focus on minimizing energy consumption. Obligations lead to more investments and make it even more expensive for first-time buyers.

230448.explanation: For us, the question remains 'does this initiate flow-through'.

230449.explanation: With current housing market prices, that is not realistic. The problem lies in having enough appropriate homes for the elderly, not in unwillingness to move.

230450.explanation: Must be appropriate and is already being provided.

230452.explanation: Also depends on the future layout.

230453.explanation: Already existing in Zeddam and Didam. The bike paths are already lit.

230455.explanation: Role for neighborhood and village councils.

230456.explanation: Evaluate the current system first before introducing a new scheme.

288608.explanation: The municipality has a participation scheme for low-income residents, but it is insufficient; swimming prevents obesity at a young age.

288609.explanation: Increase property tax with inflation correction and do not use it as an extra tax to cover financial shortfalls.

288610.explanation: Due to the tight labor market for healthcare workers, this is difficult to achieve.

288611.explanation: Provided it is for the local SME sector from Montferland, no prefab blocks.

288612.explanation: The decision to proceed with the IKC is becoming increasingly difficult. School boards and parent councils must agree with each other.

288614.explanation: There will be national expansions of the funding, but first more insight into the accountability of the subsidy.

230403.explanation: At the moment, the municipality receives sufficient resources to integrate newcomers well. The PvdA believes this group should be helped to work as soon as possible because that is the fastest way to learn Dutch.

230404.explanation: In our view, it should be mandatory for employers to house their migrant workers under the conditions of the Roemer Committee.

230405.explanation: We do not want a ban on non-sustainable companies settling, but we clearly prefer sustainable and socially responsible businesses.

230406.explanation: PvdA Montferland believes that at present it is not necessary to use agricultural land for solar panels. If this becomes necessary in the future, we support combined land use, such as solar panels with crops underneath, so that agriculture and energy generation go hand in hand.

230407.explanation: This already happens in Montferland through the Participation Scheme. We want to maintain and expand this scheme so that more residents can benefit from it.

230408.explanation: The PvdA stands for safety close to the people: visible in the neighborhood, with a strong combination of prevention, social approach, and enforcement. Investing in police and enforcement officers is necessary, always in conjunction with involved residents.

230409.explanation: PvdA Montferland considers safety important but is basically against camera surveillance in Didam. Only in exceptional situations, temporarily and targeted, and with respect for privacy.

230411.explanation: Although priority is legally limited to a maximum of 5%, we believe the municipality must do everything to help its own residents find housing in their familiar living environment.

230412.explanation: We think it is important to work with the housing association to ensure that people with a small income can continue living in our municipality. Therefore, we believe that at least 30% of new homes must be affordable.

230413.explanation: Whether it should be exactly six floors is open for discussion. But high-rise buildings should be possible. The space to build is scarce and the housing shortage is great.

230414.explanation: Absolutely not. Especially people living near the poverty line, and particularly their children, deserve support from the municipality. Getting by day-to-day is already extremely difficult; less support would be irresponsible.

230416.explanation: Informal caregiving is of great social value. With intensive informal care, applying for jobs is often not feasible and should be accommodated with understanding and tailored solutions. For lighter or limited informal care, job applications are appropriate.

230417.explanation: Youth work is an investment in prevention, opportunities, and safety. Cuts in this area will lead to bigger problems and higher costs in the long term. Youth work deserves strengthening, not budget cuts.

230419.explanation: We support expanding the number of charging points and accompanying parking spaces, in line with the growth of electric transport.

230420.explanation: Lower speeds lead to fewer accidents, more space for cyclists and pedestrians, and safer neighborhoods. Therefore, 30 km/h within built-up areas is the standard.

230421.explanation: We believe entrepreneurs with debts should have access to good debt counseling, with preserving the company as the starting point if there is prospect for continuation.

230422.explanation: The child mayor shows that children also count. This contributes to engagement and citizenship from a young age.

230423.explanation: PvdA Montferland believes it is important that residents have influence on their living environment. Therefore, we advocate for a village council and/or neighborhood council in every village or neighborhood, which can advise the municipality both on request and proactively.

230424.explanation: PvdA Montferland finds it important that residents are helped quickly and seriously. Therefore, the municipality must respond within one working day to residents' questions, at minimum with an acknowledgment of receipt and clarity about the next steps.

230425.explanation: PvdA Montferland opposes the arrival of new distribution centers. We want to protect Montferland’s landscape, livability, and traffic safety and therefore choose sustainable and appropriate spatial development. Existing agreements will be carefully completed.

230426.explanation: PvdA Montferland sees opportunities in tourism. Promotion is possible, provided it is targeted and benefits local entrepreneurs.

230427.explanation: Attracting knowledge and manufacturing industries can help retain young people, especially through interesting jobs and internships. At the same time, this is only part of the solution. Affordable housing, education, public transport connections, culture, and a rich community life are also important.

230428.explanation: PvdA Montferland believes the municipality should work as much as possible with local companies in construction and maintenance assignments, within applicable procurement rules. This strengthens the local economy and employment.

230429.explanation: PvdA Montferland is positive about cooperation with German neighboring municipalities. A joint cultural festival can contribute to connection, cultural exchange, and regional appeal.

230430.explanation: PvdA Montferland follows the Distribution Act and takes responsibility for the reception of asylum seekers.

230431.explanation: PvdA Montferland supports small-scale reception because it provides the best conditions for good guidance and support. At the same time, we acknowledge that the COA applies larger numbers to organize facilities properly.

230432.explanation: The municipality has already designated areas for wind energy. It is now time for decisive decision-making and realization.

230433.explanation: PvdA Montferland believes the Bergherbos should be protected. We reject the placement of wind turbines in this nature area; sustainable energy must not come at the expense of valuable nature and landscape.

230434.explanation: PvdA Montferland considers community centers important for livability. They must be given space to organize activities without this coming at the expense of local hospitality and entrepreneurs.

230435.explanation: PvdA Montferland supports the ambition to be energy neutral by 2035. The municipality must ensure that energy consumption by then is no higher than what is sustainably generated, with attention to feasibility and support.

230436.explanation: PvdA Montferland finds greening schoolyards important. The municipality should contribute financially to planting trees and shrubs so that schoolyards become greener, healthier, and more climate-resistant.

230437.explanation: PvdA Montferland considers greening essential for a healthy living environment. Projects are already underway to plant more trees, which we want to continue and strengthen.

230438.explanation: The municipality must cooperate in placing charging points for electric cars, thus promoting sustainable transport, while considering good distribution and the public space.

230439.explanation: PvdA Montferland finds culture and music important for young people. Therefore, more funding should go to cultural and musical activities, with accessibility for everyone as a central focus.

230443.explanation: PvdA Montferland is positive about the 1% scheme for art. By spending 1% of construction costs on art during new builds and major renovations, we invest in culture, identity, and an attractive living environment.

230445.explanation: PvdA Montferland finds long-term vacancy undesirable. The municipality should be able to fine owners who leave a building vacant for longer than a year so that properties are used again more quickly for living or working.

230446.explanation: PvdA Montferland aims for new-build homes to be as sustainable as possible. New homes should therefore generate their own energy as much as possible, for example with solar panels, in accordance with regulations and with attention to affordability.

230448.explanation: The PvdA supports homes that adapt to life. Homes suitable for different life stages: for starters, families, and the elderly. This way, people can continue living in their own village even if their situation changes, and we use the housing stock smarter and more future-proof.

230449.explanation: PvdA Montferland finds it important that the elderly can comfortably move to suitable housing. A moving allowance can help with this and ensures that large family homes become available for young families.

230450.explanation: PvdA Montferland believes the municipality should offer internships for students at all levels. This way, everyone has equal opportunities to gain work experience and get acquainted with working for the municipality.

230451.explanation: PvdA Montferland wants to relieve the historic center from through traffic. Exceptions remain possible for permit holders, in line with existing policy, with attention to accessibility and livability.

230452.explanation: PvdA Montferland wants to expand parking spaces near sports parks where necessary, while considering the environment and nature. At the same time, we want to actively encourage people to come by bike as much as possible.

230453.explanation: PvdA Montferland wants to invest in safe and sustainable cycle paths. Especially in places where cycling is limited, smart lighting that only turns on for passing cyclists can ensure safety, save energy, and reduce light pollution.

230455.explanation: Every village should have a fixed point of contact who picks up signals of loneliness among residents and-respecting privacy and working together-passes these on to the municipality, so help can be deployed timely. Above all, let's keep caring for one another.

230456.explanation: Currently, the financial resources for home care in Montferland are sufficient. Due to aging, bigger challenges await us in the future. Therefore, the PvdA wants to focus now on good organization, prevention, and sufficient care personnel so that home care remains accessible and affordable later on.

288608.explanation: Not every family can afford swimming lessons, but every child must be able to swim. The municipality must (again) allocate funds for school swimming. The municipality must take responsibility in this and ensure that no child is left behind.

288609.explanation: The property tax (ozb) is necessary to keep facilities like healthcare, education, sports, and village halls affordable. Targeted support for people with low incomes is fairer than a general reduction that mainly benefits higher incomes.

288610.explanation: Good care is personal care. People must feel seen and respected for who they are. Freedom to choose a care provider who matches their faith, culture, or beliefs contributes to trust, quality, and dignity in care.

288611.explanation: The focus should be on better utilization and sustainability of existing business parks. This way, we maintain space for housing, nature, and livability, and ensure employment that fits the scale and strength of Montferland.

288612.explanation: School buildings must be safe, healthy, and future-proof. Sometimes that means that renewing or merging is better than holding on to old buildings. It is about the quality of education, not the building itself.

288614.explanation: A library is more than just books: it is a place for learning, digital assistance, meeting, and development. For young and old. Cutting budgets here increases inequality and undermines social cohesion in Montferland.

parties.name: VVD Montferland
parties.fullName: Volkspartij voor Vrijheid en Democratie Montferland

230403.explanation: Money is not always the solution. Better use and accountability of resources.

230404.explanation: The VVD is not against housing employees on their own property but separation between housing and employment must be properly secured.

230405.explanation: Distorts the market and increases costs; it is up to the customer to decide to whom the work is awarded.

230406.explanation: Agricultural land is scarce, and there is still more than enough (roof) surface available for solar panels.

230407.explanation: There is already a participation scheme in Montferland, which works well and can be adjusted if necessary.

230408.explanation: Public order and safety are important for livability and a good society.

230409.explanation: The VVD wants to apply camera surveillance where needed; this is not predetermined for any particular town.

230411.explanation: Regional agreements are leading. More space should be created for young people originally from Montferland who want to return to where they grew up and live and work after their studies.

230412.explanation: The VVD wants affordable building according to need. This does not have to be based on quotas or municipal mandatory percentages.

230413.explanation: For the entire municipality, we must first look at the possibilities for expansion. High-rise buildings should only be considered where other options are not possible.

230414.explanation: The VVD wants to prevent that municipal poverty schemes remove the incentive for paid work (due to the poverty trap).

230416.explanation: Informal care (mantelzorg) is important but is not a standard reason not to work as well.

230417.explanation: Invest in youth in consultation with them so that the municipality remains an attractive environment.

230419.explanation: The number of parking spaces with electric charging points must be proportionate to the share of electric cars. Flexible use of these parking spaces must be investigated.

230420.explanation: This is already almost the case everywhere; the standard should be 30 km/h within built-up area boundaries.

230421.explanation: Good debt counseling prevents unnecessary bankruptcies with all the consequences for suppliers.

230422.explanation: It is good to involve youth at a young age in public governance.

230423.explanation: The obligation (must) is not necessary. The municipal council is a legally elected representative and can gather what lives in towns with or without a village council.

230424.explanation: The response time should depend on the nature of the request; good service should be the standard.

230425.explanation: The size and dimensions of business buildings must fit within the environment and can therefore be decisive; the nature of the business is not a relevant criterion.

230426.explanation: The municipality is regionally connected for marketing. Spending separate funds on advertising is not a core task of the municipality.

230427.explanation: The VVD focuses on retaining youth through more housing and better accessibility via public transport and a healthy mix of businesses, and that is more than just knowledge and manufacturing industries.

230428.explanation: Local entrepreneurs should be considered preferred suppliers and must always be able to participate in offering services.

230429.explanation: Not a task of the municipality; the VVD is in favor of stimulating initiatives.

230430.explanation: Implementation of statutory tasks should be carried out properly based on careful considerations so that local effects are minimized; speed does not contribute to this.

230431.explanation: And for a maximum of 5 years; this creates more support, keeps the shelter manageable, and makes it easier to scale down as soon as possible.

230432.explanation: The VVD does not want new search areas for wind turbines. The surroundings are unsuitable and it increases grid congestion.

230433.explanation: The Bergherbos is a valuable area that must not be destroyed by wind turbines; furthermore, the forest inherently contributes to CO2 reduction through the existing trees.

230434.explanation: But only on the condition that it is done in good harmony with the local hospitality sector and that it is not mixed with government subsidies.

230435.explanation: This is only feasible with the application of a local small power plant. Otherwise, the 2035 ambition is unachievable and also unnecessary because there are sufficient areas where much more energy is generated than consumed.

230436.explanation: Planting additional trees must be done in good consultation with the immediate surroundings, and maintenance must also be taken into account.

230437.explanation: The VVD believes that the planting of greenery with trees should be targeted where it fits and is functional. It is not a goal in itself.

230438.explanation: The VVD believes the placement of charging stations can be left to the market; the municipality facilitates based on a good coverage plan proportional to the share of electric transport.

230439.explanation: Not specifically for young people; the VVD wants to generally invest more in arts and culture, but in cooperation with society and entrepreneurs.

230443.explanation: According to the VVD, this increases costs for municipal procurement funded by public money.

230445.explanation: Unnecessary vacancy must be prevented; (temporary) relaxation of rules for the use of vacant properties can also help reduce vacancy.

230446.explanation: Homes must comply with the national building code; the VVD opposes additional local regulations and restrictions that increase costs. As an owner, one can very well decide for themselves what they want. The municipality does not need to interfere.

230448.explanation: There must always be customized solutions, so building what is locally needed. Construction should be based on demand instead of steering by fixed percentages.

230449.explanation: Moving costs are not the barrier for elderly moving to smaller homes. There must be sufficient smaller homes available for the elderly, so build, build, build.

230450.explanation: Students must intern in positions that fit the municipality’s and student’s needs. The municipality as an employer is one among many. MBO internships can be arranged anywhere; that is up to the students themselves.

230451.explanation: For retailers in 's-Heerenberg, good accessibility by car is very important for survival. A good and well-thought traffic circulation plan helps with this.

230452.explanation: Parking pressure in this recreation and sports area is high, which endangers traffic safety.

230453.explanation: Further research on dynamic lighting’s pros and cons is needed; if applied, only at natural replacement moments.

230455.explanation: We see a role for Welcom here, potentially in cooperation with neighborhood and village councils. The municipality does not need to establish anything separate for this.

230456.explanation: The funds Montferland receives from the national government should cover this. More effective use helps. Allocating extra local money would come at the expense of general social provisions.

288608.explanation: Not specifically for school swimming; the contribution for physical education must be maintained, schools decide themselves how to spend it.

288609.explanation: The municipal organization must become smaller so that taxes can be reduced.

288610.explanation: If the municipality has to bear care costs, then it may determine who provides this care.

288611.explanation: More industrial estates must be developed in Montferland. Employment is the foundation for a strong economy and less poverty. The municipality must enable this.

288612.explanation: The municipality must continue using existing school buildings. With appropriate investments, better accessible and good educational facilities can be realized instead of the €32 million now needed for a new Integrated Child Center at a difficult-to-reach location.

288614.explanation: The library should generate more of its own income instead of relying on the municipality’s subsidy.

230403.explanation: It is important that newcomers immediately participate in Montferland society. This requires investment in integration, language lessons, and introduction to local customs. With paid jobs or volunteer work, they contribute to the local economy.

230404.explanation: It is important that labor migrants have good housing. It is a positive thing that employers can provide this themselves, close to the workplace.

230405.explanation: Companies that operate sustainably should be given priority over polluting companies. When a polluting or non-sustainable company settles, agreements are made on how the company can improve its work processes. Local employment is important to D66.

230406.explanation: All opportunities to make Montferland energy neutral, in accordance with (regional) agreements, must be utilized.

230407.explanation: This already happens via the Montferland participation scheme. We want to maintain this scheme.

230408.explanation: Safety is a task for the police. The municipality of Montferland will not allocate its own additional resources for this. However, a critical look can be taken at where municipal enforcement officers are deployed.

230409.explanation: Cameras are a heavy measure and must be proportional to a problem. There is no problem in Didam and therefore no reason to constantly monitor people.

230411.explanation: There are already regional agreements. Priority is given to people from the Achterhoek/Liemers region. The existing scheme is fine.

230412.explanation: This is already established in a scheme. We will continue these agreements and monitor progress.

230413.explanation: There is a need for more housing. D66 prefers to build within existing centers, with higher housing density rather than expanding into rural areas. New construction must align with the needs of Montferland residents.

230414.explanation: D66 stands for an inclusive society where no one is left behind. That is why we continue to support Montferlanders with vulnerable financial positions without reduction.

230416.explanation: The abolition of the job application requirement should be assessed individually. Every person and every caregiving situation is different.

230417.explanation: By investing preventively in young people, bigger problems are avoided. Youth work is a low-threshold basic facility that guides young people in their free time as they grow towards adulthood.

230419.explanation: Not everyone has the possibility to charge an electric car at home. There must be space for this in public areas.

230420.explanation: This is already part of the current policy. With a lower maximum speed for motorized traffic, the reaction time to a situation is longer and the chance of injury or fatality is smaller. Therefore, where it improves safety, we reduce the maximum speed to 30 km/h.

230421.explanation: If a business is still viable, there are possibilities. Depending on the situation, the entrepreneur can be offered assistance. This is tailor-made.

230422.explanation: The children’s mayor contributes to raising political awareness among children. D66 wants to expand this role with a youth council to amplify the opinions and ideas of 13 to 18-year-olds.

230423.explanation: There are also other forms where residents can be consulted for advice: for example, online surveys or through other resident groups (neighborhood connectors). Not all villages have a village council that represents the residents well.

230424.explanation: A resident has the right to a response within one working day. If the question cannot be answered immediately, the resident is informed when the answer will be provided.

230425.explanation: In 's-Heerenberg, DocksNLD2 will still be realized within the agreed frameworks, including landscape integration. There will be no other business parks for large-scale distribution centers.

230426.explanation: Agreements have already been made on this in the Recreation and Tourism policy and funds have been reserved for it. The money generated from the tourist tax is invested in promotion.

230427.explanation: If you want to be an attractive residential municipality for young people and starters, suitable jobs must also be available in the region.

230428.explanation: Local procurement is preferred. The municipality must still comply with the statutory procurement rules.

230429.explanation: Montferland must invest in regional cooperation, including cooperation with German neighboring municipalities. A cultural festival focusing on connection and meeting can help with this.

230430.explanation: Montferland has a legal obligation to accommodate asylum seekers and has made regional agreements with its neighboring municipalities about this. Montferland must take its responsibility.

230431.explanation: At larger asylum seeker centers, facilities (education, care, and safety) can be organized better. This will not come at the expense of care and education for the Montferland population. Also, there is extra support from the COA at larger locations.

230432.explanation: The wind turbine locations have already been designated by the municipal council in the previous council period.

230433.explanation: The Bergherbos is not a search area for wind turbines. The installation of wind turbines in the Bergherbos is NOT possible according to current policy.

230434.explanation: The municipality of Montferland sold village halls mostly for 1 euro. Clear agreements were made at that time about para-commercial activities. Agreement is agreement: village halls may not become competitors to commercial hospitality businesses.

230435.explanation: D66 supports the ambition: a climate-neutral 2035. Montferland must therefore facilitate, motivate, and encourage residents to make this goal possible.

230436.explanation: The more trees plan in Montferland, initiated by D66, is already successfully being implemented. If schools want to invest in green schoolyards, the municipality supports that.

230437.explanation: The more trees plan in Montferland, initiated by D66, is already successfully underway. It remains a continuous process to preserve or replace existing trees and plant new ones. Trees play an important role in cooling public spaces and houses.

230438.explanation: There are more and more electric cars. Not everyone has the possibility to charge the car on their own property. Therefore, there must be sufficient public charging stations available.

230439.explanation: It is important for the development of children and young people to come into contact with culture and music. Not everyone gets this at home. The municipality ensures that all children and young people can be exposed to this.

230443.explanation: This scheme already exists for new construction projects by the government.

230445.explanation: There can be understandable circumstances why a house is empty for more than a year. How can this be enforced? There is insufficient capacity for that.

230446.explanation: Residents must be encouraged and supported to generate their own energy. The municipality should also encourage residents or companies to set up energy communities themselves.

230448.explanation: We implement the agreement to realize at least 30% of new construction as life-course resilient homes. This is already laid down in the Housing Vision and the performance agreements with Plavei.

230449.explanation: D66 wants to increase the flow in the housing market. A moving bonus can encourage seniors living in large (social rental) homes to move to smaller ones. Senior advisors and moving/home coaches also play an important role.

230450.explanation: The municipality must take its social responsibility by offering sufficient internship places for all education levels.

230451.explanation: The center must remain accessible, but that does not mean that cars should be given space everywhere. Sufficient parking options along the edges of the center are necessary. Destination traffic in the center must remain possible.

230452.explanation: A clear vision must first be developed for the entire Nevelhorst area. Promising more parking spaces in advance is undesirable.

230453.explanation: The municipality must encourage people to use the bicycle more. Good lighting is part of this and is essential.

230455.explanation: The municipality must pay more attention to the theme of loneliness and set up a central reporting point itself. Signals can come from all kinds of people (professionals, family, neighbors, etc.). The formation of precautionary circles is encouraged.

230456.explanation: The starting point is that every senior receives the appropriate care needed. More customization and prevention should ensure that municipal Wmo expenditures are kept under control. From 2027, the personal contribution is expected to be income-dependent again.

288608.explanation: Schools themselves decide on the content of their sports lessons. Swimming is a basic skill, but parents can arrange that themselves. And for those who cannot afford it financially, there are schemes.

288609.explanation: The municipality has more and more tasks to perform and faces rising costs and uncertain national contributions. We must ensure that we do not spend more than necessary, but cuts are not expected.

288610.explanation: Receiving quality care is paramount. The professionalism of the caregiver must be guaranteed.

288611.explanation: Local employment is important. There are already agreements about the development of DocksNLD2, including space for local entrepreneurs. This plan must be realized. Regionally, it will be considered where more business parks can be possible, and this does not have to be in Montferland.

288612.explanation: All children have the right to education in good classrooms that meet all requirements. The Education Housing Plan records which schools need new buildings and which need renovations. This is tailor-made. A new broad school must be built in 's-Heerenberg.

288614.explanation: The library is more than a place where you can borrow books. It is an accessible knowledge and meeting center for all residents because everyone should be able to develop themselves, regardless of age or income.

230403.explanation: The PVV believes the municipality should not spend a single euro on the integration of newcomers.

230404.explanation: The PVV believes the municipality should not cooperate in housing labor migrants on business parks. This can lead to nuisance and extra pressure on the living environment and municipal facilities.

230405.explanation: The PVV believes the municipality of Montferland should not impose sustainability requirements on companies that want to establish themselves in the municipality. Entrepreneurship and employment should not be hindered by excessively green ideological demands.

230406.explanation: The PVV is against solar panels on valuable agricultural land. Agricultural land is meant for food production, not for energy generation.

230407.explanation: The PVV believes the municipality should not pay for sports memberships. Montferland already has the program 'Participate in Montferland for targeted support.' New or additional schemes are not necessary.

230408.explanation: The PVV wants more municipal enforcement officers in Montferland. Visible enforcement is necessary to tackle nuisance and crime. Enforcement officers must act decisively and not just issue warnings or reprimands.

230409.explanation: The PVV believes that camera surveillance in Didam should be possible if there is reason for it. It must be used purposefully for specific safety issues and periodically evaluated.

230411.explanation: For the PVV, residents of Montferland always have priority when obtaining social rental housing. In addition, we ensure that status holders no longer receive priority when obtaining social rental housing, not even with urgency.

230412.explanation: The PVV does not choose fixed percentages for new construction. A mandatory 30% social rental housing per project is too rigid and can delay construction. The composition of homes must match local needs.

230413.explanation: The PVV supports high-rise buildings in Didam. Due to the housing shortage, it is necessary to use available space more efficiently.

230414.explanation: The PVV believes poverty policy should focus on people who truly need it. The municipality must carefully define support to ensure help reaches those living in poverty and schemes are not deployed too broadly.

230416.explanation: The PVV believes that welfare recipients who provide intensive informal care should not be required to apply for jobs by default. Informal care is socially valuable. At the same time, each situation must be carefully assessed to prevent abuse.

230417.explanation: The PVV believes youth work can play an important role in preventing nuisance and providing structure to young people. Therefore, cuts are not appropriate; youth work must be purposeful, practical, and visibly effective.

230419.explanation: The PVV believes the municipality should be cautious in designating extra parking spaces for electric cars. Parking spots are scarce in Montferland and should remain available for everyone, regardless of vehicle type.

230420.explanation: The PVV finds the current maximum speed limit inside built-up areas acceptable. A general reduction to 30 km/h is unnecessary and mainly aims to frustrate motorists.

230421.explanation: The PVV believes entrepreneurs should be able to use municipal debt counseling without having to immediately close their business. Where recovery is possible, business continuity should be part of the process.

230422.explanation: The PVV considers the children's mayor a symbolic role that is unnecessarily institutionalized.

230423.explanation: Each village has different wishes and interests. The municipal council is the highest governing body and is elected by all residents from all villages. Village councils add an extra unelected administrative layer.

230424.explanation: The PVV believes the municipality should respond to residents carefully and accurately. A mandatory reply within one working day is not always realistic and may harm quality. However, the municipality must communicate clearly and timely about case handling.

230425.explanation: The PVV is against new distribution centers in Montferland. The municipality already has enough of them; therefore, we consider further industrialization of Montferland undesirable.

230426.explanation: The PVV believes the municipality should be cautious with advertising campaigns for tourism. Small-scale tourism can fit Montferland, but large-scale promotion using public funds is not desirable.

230427.explanation: The PVV believes binding young people does not depend on attracting specific sectors such as knowledge and manufacturing industries. What mainly binds young people is the ability to find affordable and suitable housing. The PVV is committed to that.

230428.explanation: The PVV believes the municipality should focus on local businesses for construction and maintenance contracts to strengthen the local economy and keep community funds within the municipality as much as possible.

230429.explanation: The PVV wants to strengthen economic and cultural cooperation with neighboring German municipalities. A joint cultural festival can contribute to this, provided it remains limited in scale and costs and leaves room for associations and local initiatives.

230430.explanation: The PVV wants no asylum seeker reception center in Montferland. Not temporary, not structural, and not under pressure from The Hague. The municipality must stand up for its own residents and offer no space for asylum reception!

230431.explanation: The PVV is against asylum reception in Montferland, regardless of group size. We absolutely do not want reception in smaller groups either. The municipality should not accommodate asylum seekers at all and should end any existing asylum reception as soon as possible.

230432.explanation: The PVV is against designating areas for wind turbines in Montferland. Wind turbines damage the landscape, cause nuisance, and lead to health problems for residents nearby.

230433.explanation: The placement of wind turbines in the Bergherbos must be prevented. The PVV does not want wind turbines in our beautiful Bergherbos!

230434.explanation: The PVV believes village halls should have more space to generate their own income. By organizing parties and activities more often, they can remain financially healthy and maintain their social function for the village.

230435.explanation: The PVV believes the municipality should not set a goal for balancing energy consumption and generation. Residents and entrepreneurs should not be pressured to use less energy continuously. Reliable and affordable energy should be the priority.

230436.explanation: If schools want to green their schoolyards, they must finance this themselves.

230437.explanation: The PVV believes planting more trees should not be an end in itself. The municipality should focus on proper management of the existing greenery in our municipality.

230438.explanation: The PVV believes the municipality should not cooperate in placing more charging stations for electric cars. Parking spaces in our municipality are already scarce enough.

230439.explanation: The PVV does not want to spend more public funds on culture and music, regardless of the target group.

230443.explanation: The PVV opposes a mandatory 1% scheme for art. This is pure nonsense that makes construction projects more expensive and adds nothing to a building's utility.

230445.explanation: The PVV wants to tackle vacancy in the municipality of Montferland. Fines and levies are among the options to address this.

230446.explanation: The PVV opposes the requirement that new homes must generate their own energy. This worsens grid congestion, drives up construction costs, and delays housing development. Residents of Montferland should not be forced into specific energy solutions.

230448.explanation: The PVV does not choose fixed percentages for new construction. A mandatory 30% norm per project is too rigid and can delay building. There must be sufficient homes for the elderly but not via a mandatory 30% norm per project.

230449.explanation: After a lifetime of hard work, the PVV believes elderly people should simply be able to continue living where they live. Moving on should never pressure the elderly and must always be voluntary.

230450.explanation: The PVV believes more MBO (vocational education) students should be able to do internships at the municipality. This mainly involves practical and executive work so young people can gain work experience.

230451.explanation: The center of ’s-Heerenberg must remain easily accessible by car, just like the other village centers. Many residents and entrepreneurs depend on cars. Accessibility and sufficient parking are essential for livability and the local economy. The PVV stands for this!

230452.explanation: The PVV believes more parking spaces should be created at De Nevelhorst. This improves accessibility to the recreational area, especially for families and the elderly.

230453.explanation: The PVV believes the municipality should not invest extra money in smart lighting for bike paths. Good basic lighting is sufficient and should be well maintained by the municipality.

230455.explanation: The PVV acknowledges loneliness as a problem, especially among the elderly, but we believe this should not be solved through fixed contact persons or reporting structures per village organized by the municipality. Social cohesion arises through family, neighbors, associations, and community spirit.

230456.explanation: The PVV firmly supports good home care for the elderly. Seniors should be able to rely on timely and humane care. However, more money is not always the solution; healthcare funding must mainly be spent better and more efficiently.

288608.explanation: Being able to swim is very important in the Netherlands because our country is inextricably linked to water. The PVV believes school swimming should be supported, with contributions from both the municipality and schools.

288609.explanation: The PVV believes that the property tax should be lowered. Residents and entrepreneurs already pay enough fixed costs. The municipality should be frugal with public funds and reduce charges instead of continually increasing them.

288610.explanation: The PVV believes care must focus on quality, availability, and professionalism. Religion, culture, or belief should not play a role in the allocation of care. Care should not have an ideological or identity-driven approach.

288611.explanation: The PVV believes Montferland should not be further built up with new business parks. The focus should be on better utilizing existing sites and vacancies so that the landscape and livability are preserved and urban sprawl prevented.

288612.explanation: The PVV believes the municipality should continue using existing school buildings if suitable. This avoids unnecessary costs and new construction and uses space and resources efficiently.

288614.explanation: The PVV believes Montferland should not cut back on the library. The library plays an important role in literacy, education, information, and social interaction, especially for youth and the elderly. This is a basic facility that must be maintained.

230403.explanation: The municipality carries out its statutory tasks, but additional expenditures for integration should primarily be funded by the national government and must not come at the expense of local facilities.

230404.explanation: We oppose fragmented housing on business parks and choose centralized housing at suitable locations with sufficient support and facilities.

230405.explanation: Companies already meet legal sustainability requirements and municipal policies for new establishments. We ensure sustainability through clear standards, not through a general establishment ban.

230406.explanation: We are against routinely allowing solar panels on agricultural land. We first use roofs and existing locations; only in exceptional cases is customized solutions possible.

230407.explanation: Sports increase opportunities, health, and participation. Support for children from low-income families prevents bigger problems and costs later in life.

230408.explanation: More enforcement officers (BOAs) lead to better enforcement of existing rules and contribute to livability, safety, and residents' trust.

230409.explanation: Camera surveillance is a serious measure that should only be used when demonstrable problems exist and based on clear justification, not unnecessarily or solely for one neighborhood.

230411.explanation: Housing allocation is not a municipal task. Through performance agreements, we steer on urgency and need, not on general priority for local residents.

230412.explanation: The 30/30/30 distribution is an important principle for the municipality’s total housing development. However, per project, customization must remain possible if there are good and substantiated reasons. Rigid standards per plan do not do justice to differences between locations.

230413.explanation: The maximum building height will be set in the environmental plan to be adopted by the municipal council. If that plan allows high-rise buildings up to six floors, that is possible. If the plan does not allow it, it is not possible. The consideration belongs in the environmental plan, not in a general yes/no statement.

230414.explanation: Lowering the income threshold increases poverty and leads to health and social problems. This is undesirable and more costly in the long run.

230416.explanation: Informal care is often intensive and time-consuming. For people receiving social assistance benefits who demonstrably provide informal care, we see this as a form of socially valuable commitment. They therefore cannot be required to also apply for jobs simultaneously.

230417.explanation: Youth work is preventive and especially essential after the corona period. Cuts lead to bigger problems; however, assessment of effectiveness and efficiency is needed.

230419.explanation: A good and balanced distribution of charging points is necessary to enable electric driving. Location choices must be carefully made, in consultation with residents and considering accessibility and livability.

230420.explanation: A 30 km/h speed limit improves safety and livability but is already the norm in many neighborhoods. Therefore, the statement adds little new.

230421.explanation: Debt counseling must also be accessible to entrepreneurs so that viable businesses can be preserved. This is done under clear conditions and with predetermined principles. The scheme is intended for local entrepreneurs, not larger companies.

230422.explanation: The children’s mayor contributes to citizenship education and involving children in local democracy and thus has clear added value.

230423.explanation: Village councils work best when they originate from the grassroots. Obligations without support add little value; the municipality can, however, actively encourage initiatives.

230424.explanation: A quick response is essential. Within one working day, it must be clear how and when a request will be handled, and agreements must then be kept.

230425.explanation: Existing agreements about ’s-Heerenberg-East must be honored. After completion, expansion is not likely, but we find a general ban on distribution centers too restrictive.

230426.explanation: Targeted marketing strengthens recreation and tourism and thus the local economy. Regional cooperation is important, but Montferland’s own profile remains necessary.

230427.explanation: Attracting knowledge and manufacturing industries is important but alone insufficient to retain young people in Montferland. Suitable housing, facilities, and opportunities for socializing and recreation are also needed.

230428.explanation: Local businesses deserve opportunities, but procurement rules limit giving priority. Where allowed, we utilize that space; where not, we comply with the law.

230429.explanation: We welcome cross-border cooperation, but not limited to one mandatory cultural festival. In the euregional context, we prefer broader collaboration across multiple fields.

230430.explanation: The municipality has a responsibility to contribute to the reception of asylum seekers. The municipal council has established a careful process and clear frameworks for this. That process has started and must be executed decisively and as agreed.

230431.explanation: The number of asylum seekers per location must depend on the place and circumstances. A fixed upper limit of 100 is too rigid and hinders customization.

230432.explanation: Locations have already been designated and regional agreements made. First, implement what has been agreed before designating new areas.

230433.explanation: The Bergherbos is a valuable nature and recreation area. Installing wind turbines does not fit here and lacks broad support among residents. We choose to generate sustainable energy at other, more suitable locations.

230434.explanation: More space for income helps village halls remain vital, provided this is done with customization, consultation, and without unfair competition for local hospitality businesses.

230435.explanation: Energy neutrality is desirable, but due to infrastructure and grid capacity limitations, a hard deadline in 2035 is not realistic and cannot be fully managed by the municipality.

230436.explanation: Green policies have already been established and financed and are now being implemented. Additional contributions beyond this program are currently financially unjustifiable.

230437.explanation: Tree policies have already been established and are currently being implemented. First execute and evaluate before agreeing on further expansion.

230438.explanation: Cooperating on charging stations supports electric driving, provided locations are carefully chosen and in consultation with local residents.

230439.explanation: Supporting policies and funding already exist. Whether additional money is needed will be determined based on evaluation and set goals, not automatically.

230443.explanation: Art and culture require tailored approaches. A mandatory 1% scheme per building is too rigid and inefficient.

230445.explanation: Vacancy is not always blameworthy and legally difficult to sanction. We prefer encouraging reuse over punishment.
