230446.explanation: New homes must already comply with strict national energy standards. Additional municipal obligations are legally and practically undesirable; encouragement works better than mandates.

230448.explanation: Housing turnover is important, but a fixed 30% quota for seniors is too rigid and unfeasible. Tailored solutions per project work better.

230449.explanation: A relocation allowance can voluntarily encourage seniors to trade a large family home for a suitable dwelling. This promotes housing turnover. However, costs and effects must be studied beforehand.

230450.explanation: More internship placements are desirable, but their implementation is the responsibility of the municipal organization, not the council.

230451.explanation: An attractive and livable center is important, provided it is also accessible for entrepreneurs, residents, and visitors. A low-car center can contribute to tourism and safety if entrepreneurs/residents maintain access and sufficient parking is available in or near the center.

230452.explanation: To make Nevelhorst more attractive for residents and visitors, infrastructure must grow with the development. This includes sufficient and well-integrated parking facilities, aligned with the nature and scale of the revitalization.

230453.explanation: Smart lighting on bike paths can improve safety, especially in rural areas. The integrated mobility plan should inventory where this is most effective. Installation can be phased as needed, within financial possibilities.

230455.explanation: Loneliness requires a coherent and professional approach within the social domain. Various instruments and involved professionals are already active. Assigning one contact person per village is too limited and does not do justice to the complexity of the problem.

230456.explanation: Good home care is essential. Extra resources are negotiable if care demand increases or the current supply is insufficient.

288608.explanation: Montferland offers sufficient opportunities for swimming lessons outside school hours. Targeted municipal support is available for families who cannot afford the lesson fees.

288609.explanation: The property tax (ozb) has not increased over recent years, except by the statutory price indexation. Further reduction is therefore unrealistic without consequences for facilities.

288610.explanation: Although respect for everyone’s background is important, within Montferland’s size and capacity it is organizationally and practically not feasible to always guarantee freedom of choice by faith or culture without extra pressure on care.

288611.explanation: With the planned realization of the industrial site ’s-Heerenberg East, Montferland has reached maximum capacity. The focus should be on better utilization and greening of existing sites, not constant new expansions.

288612.explanation: The decision to realize an Integral Child Center (IKC) in ’s-Heerenberg was deliberate. Holding on to the current fragmented school locations leads to insurmountable organizational and educational problems and is not a future-proof solution.

288614.explanation: The library provides an essential social function for education, meeting, and development. As part of the IKC, it is indispensable for the Montferland community and must not be cut.

statements.title: Every village should have a village council that advises the municipality.
No new distribution centers should be allowed in Montferland.

titleParts.glossary: Large warehouses where goods are received, sorted and transported to other locations.

statements.title: Montferland should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: If a resident asks the municipality a question, the municipality must respond within one working day.
If employers want to house labor migrants on their own premises, the municipality should cooperate as much as possible.
There should be camera surveillance in Didam.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: More money should go to culture and music for young people.
The municipality should invest in an advertising campaign to attract more tourists.
The municipality should designate areas for wind turbines as soon as possible.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet zo snel mogelijk gebieden aanwijzen waar

titleParts.glossary: These are modern wind turbines that convert wind power into electricity and can be up to 150 meters high.

statements.title: More MBO students (vocational education) should be able to do internships at the municipality.

titleParts.text: moeten stage kunnen lopen bij de gemeente.

statements.title: The municipality should provide accommodation for asylum seekers as soon as possible.
Many more trees should be planted in Montferland.
The municipality should invest more in cycle paths with smart lighting that only turns on when cyclists pass.
There should be more parking spaces at De Nevelhorst.
The municipality must fine owners who leave a building vacant for longer than a year.
High-rise buildings up to six stories may be allowed in Didam.

titleParts.glossary: Buildings with homes or businesses on multiple floors, for example apartments or flats.

statements.title: The center of 's-Heerenberg should remain easily accessible by car.
Each village should have a contact person who notifies the municipality if residents become socially isolated.
Montferland should spend more money on home care for the elderly.

titleParts.text: Montferland moet meer geld uitgeven aan

titleParts.glossary: Care provided at home by staff with a diploma (qualified staff).

statements.title: More business parks should be developed in Montferland.
The municipality must ensure that by 2035 Montferland consumes no more energy than it generates.
People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: New homes should generate their own energy, for example with solar panels.
The municipality should continue to use the existing school buildings.
There must be a relocation allowance for elderly people who leave a large family home.
The municipality should organize a cultural festival together with neighboring German municipalities.

shootoutStatements.title: The municipality should spend more money on integrating newcomers into society.
People receiving welfare benefits who provide informal care to a family member or acquaintance do not have to apply for jobs.
When allocating social housing, applicants from the municipality should be given priority.
The municipality should contribute to the planting of trees and shrubs on schoolyards.
Entrepreneurs must be able to apply for debt counseling while keeping their business.
The municipality should abolish the child mayor.
In new developments of more than ten homes at least 30% must be social rental housing.
The municipality should accommodate asylum seekers in groups of no more than 100 people.
To retain young people in Montferland the municipality should attract more knowledge and manufacturing industries.
From the construction costs of a building, 1% should from now on be spent on art.
Village community centers should be allowed to earn more money by holding parties more often.
The municipality should not cooperate in installing charging stations for electric cars.
The municipality must allow solar panels on agricultural land.
Of all new construction, 30% should be designated for elderly people.
The municipality should prevent the placement of wind turbines in Bergherbos.
The municipality must pay for sports club memberships for children whose parents or guardians have little money.
For construction and maintenance contracts the municipality should give priority to local companies.
The municipality should support fewer people in poverty by lowering the income threshold for assistance.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.

shootoutStatements.theme: The municipality should (again) allocate funds for school swimming.
shootoutStatements.title: The municipality should once again provide funding for school swimming.
The municipality should cut spending on youth work.
Only companies that operate sustainably should be allowed to establish themselves in our municipality from now on.

shootoutStatements.theme: Montferland must not cut funding for the library.
shootoutStatements.title: Montferland must not cut funding for the library.
